current: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) (sharp converse, thm:minimax-lower, all gamma) and r_feas the DERIVED plain-clipped-AIPW exponent (oeq:feasible-upper), r_feas<=r_star with equality at gamma=0. FIRST welfare-regret rate under weak (one-sided polynomial) overlap, extending strict-overlap policy learning. The r_star-r_feas gap (positive for gamma>0) is the weak-overlap nuisance-estimation cost; tightness is open (oeq:feasible-tight).
proposed: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, the standing feasible-estimator conditions used in oeq:feasible-upper, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), and gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) and r_feas the derived plain-clipped-AIPW exponent. Moreover r_feas<=r_star, with equality at gamma=0 and more generally whenever kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma). The strict positive gap occurs precisely in the nuisance-limited weak-overlap branch kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma); feasible tightness is resolved in the equal-exponent branch and remains open in the strict-gap branch (oeq:feasible-tight).
reason: The displayed two-sided rate bracket is proved. The informal clause saying the gap is positive for every gamma>0 is too strong under the corrected def:feasible-rate: if the nuisance exponents are strong enough that kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma), then r_feas=min{r_star,kappa zeta}=r_star even when gamma>0. The tightness statement must therefore be localized to the strict-gap branch rather than all weak-overlap regimes.
